revision.text:
'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Harai-no-Ōji|ja|祓戸王子}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yunomine Ōji}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of revision.text:
'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Harai-no-Ōji|ja|祓戸王子}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Harai-no-Ōji|ja|祓戸王子}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Harai-no-Ōji|ja|祓戸王子}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yunomine Ōji}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 1]] revision.text:
'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Harai-no-Ōji|ja|祓戸王子}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yunomine Ōji}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Harai-no-Ōji|ja|祓戸王子}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yunomine Ōji}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Harai-no-Ōji|ja|祓戸王子}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Harai-no-Ōji|ja|祓戸王子}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Harai-no-Ōji|ja|祓戸王子}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} * {{ill|Kujūku revision.text:
'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子}}/{{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|lt=Hongū Taisha}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yunomine Ōji}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:11]] '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子}}/{{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|lt=Hongū Taisha}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yunomine Ōji}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]

It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji (田辺市本宮町)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji (田辺市本宮町)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子}}/{{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|lt=Hongū Taisha}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yunomine Ōji}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of revision.text:
'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji (田辺市本宮町)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子}}/{{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|lt=Hongū Taisha}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yunomine Ōji}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路}} * {{ill|Kujūku The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅|12=draft}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|12=draft}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|12=draft}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅|12=draft}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|12=draft}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅|12=draft}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村|12=draft}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|12=draft}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}} - [[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|12=draft}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|12=draft}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|12=draft}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|12=draft}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家|12=draft}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川|12=draft}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|12=draft}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮|12=draft}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|12=draft}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅|12=draft}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|12=draft}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|12=draft}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅|12=draft}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|12=draft}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅|12=draft}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村|12=draft}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|12=draft}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|12=draft}}/{{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|lt=Hongū Taisha|12=draft}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉|12=draft}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yunomine Ōji}} ==[[Kujūku Ōji template|List of the 99 Oji]]== {{citation needed|湯の峰王子|ill|Jōnan-gū|en}}{{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社|lt=Nachi Taisha|lt=11|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}} ]] {{Kujūku Ōji}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji|-てんふれえと]] [[Category:s11]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[en:Yunomine Ōji]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yunomine Ōji}}

The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅|12=draft}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|12=draft}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|12=draft}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅|12=draft}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|12=draft}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅|12=draft}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村|12=draft}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|12=draft}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}} - The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}/{{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|lt=Hongū Taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yunomine Ōji}} ==[[Kujūku Ōji template|List of the 99 Oji]]== {{citation needed|湯の峰王子|ill|Jōnan-gū|en}}{{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社|lt=Nachi Taisha|lt=11|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft}} {{Kujūku Ōji}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[en:Yunomine Ōji]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yunomine Ōji}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yunomine Ōji}}

The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. revision.comment: 53 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:湯の峰王子 up to rev 98184098
revision.text:
[[Image:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|湯の峰王子社祠]] [[Image:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|王子社鳥居]] '''湯の峰王子'''（ゆのみねおうじ）は[[和歌山県]][[田辺市]]本宮町湯峰にある[[神社]]。[[九十九王子]]のひとつで、[[湯の峰温泉]]の温泉街にほど近い、[[東光寺 (田辺市)|東光寺]]の裏手の丘の上にある。国の[[史跡]]「熊野参詣道」（[[2000年]]〈[[平成]]12年〉11月2日指定）の一部<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=田辺市の指定文化財 －記念物－ |publisher=田辺市教育委員会 |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}</ref>。
== 概要 == 湯の峰王子は九十九王子のひとつに数えられるものの、例えば[[藤原定家]]の参詣記に記述がないなど、中世熊野詣期の参詣記にはほとんど記録されていない。この時期の参詣道が、[[本宮町 (和歌山県)|熊野本宮]]から[[熊野川]]を[[新宮市|新宮]]へと下っており、ルートから外れていること、また、[[熊野本宮大社]]参拝に先立っての湯垢離儀礼による潔斎がこの時期には行われていないためである<ref name="Kumanoji">熊野路編さん委員会[1973: 155]</ref>。
ただ、湯の峰を中世熊野詣の参詣者が訪れなかったわけではない。[[天仁]]2年（[[1109年]]）、[[藤原宗忠]]は、[[熊野三山]]巡拝後に船で本宮に帰着した折に湯の峰に立ち寄り、万病を除く名湯と賞賛している（『[[中右記]]』）が、これは長旅の疲れを癒すためであり、信仰上の意義が存するわけではない。[[1134年]]（[[長承]]3年）には、[[鳥羽天皇|鳥羽院]]の御幸に同行した[[藤原璋子|待賢門院]]は、鳥羽院一行の本宮着が遅れている間に、休養のために湯の峰を訪ねようとしたが、鳥羽院一行が来着したため断念している<ref name="Kumanoji"/>。
史料初出は、鎌倉時代末、[[正中 (元号)|正中]]3年（[[1326年]]）の[[仁和寺]]文書『熊野縁起』に湯峰童子とあるもので、次いで[[文明 (日本)|文明]]5年（[[1474年]]）の『王子記』に「湯峯王子」の記載が見える<ref name="Heibon">平凡社[1997: 794]</ref> が、いずれも中世熊野詣の盛期とは大きく離れている。湯垢離儀礼による潔斎の定着もこの時期以降と考えられており、儀礼の定着に伴って新たに設けられたものと考えられている<ref name="Heibon"/>。その一例として、[[足利義満]]の側室、北野殿らの一行による参詣記に、日中に本宮に到着してから、夜に行われる奉幣の前に湯の峰を訪れている例がある<ref name="Kumanoji"/> ほか、[[熊野本宮大社例大祭]]の湯登神事（毎年4月13日）で今日でも行われている湯の峰温泉での湯垢離は、[[江戸時代]]には記載が見られる（『紀伊続風土記』）<ref name="Heibon"/>。
[[室町時代|室町期]]ころから熊野詣が衰勢し、かわって盛んになった[[西国三十三所]]の一部に中辺路が組み込まれるようになると、参詣者たちは、[[熊野那智大社]]から[[雲取越え]]を行い、温泉での休息後、しばしば[[熊野本宮大社|熊野本宮]]へ立ち寄ることなく[[九十九王子 (田辺市中辺路町)#三越峠と赤木越|赤木越]]経由で[[九十九王子 (田辺市中辺路町)#三越峠と赤木越|三越峠]]へ連絡し、[[田辺市|紀伊田辺]]方面へ直接帰路をとるようになった<ref name="Kumanoji"/><ref name="Nishi">西[1987: 166]</ref>。
近世以降の記録にはときとして名が見られ、『[[紀伊続風土記]]』には社領5石、修造は官営によるとの記述があるほか、本宮大社の管理下にある末社であったとも記されている<ref name="Heibon"/><ref name="Nishi"/>。もとは東光寺の境内、寺堂の右にあったが、[[1903年]]（[[明治36年]]）[[5月]]の火災で、東光寺の本堂ともに焼失、現在地に再建された<ref name="Nishi"/>。
== 交通機関 == * [[紀勢本線]][[新宮駅]]より[[熊野交通]]バスで約60分 * [[紀勢本線]][[紀伊田辺駅]]より[[龍神自動車|龍神バス]]で約90分 * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[大和八木駅]]より、[[奈良交通]]の[[新宮駅]]ゆき特急バス、近鉄[[高田市駅]]、[[近鉄御所駅]]、[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[和歌山線]][[五条駅 (奈良県)|五条駅]]、[[十津川村|十津川]]を経由、約5時間20分 いずれも「湯の峰温泉」バス停で下車
== 所在地 == 和歌山県田辺市本宮町湯峰11
== 注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book ja-jp|author=熊野路編さん委員会|year=1973|title=古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路|publisher=熊野中辺路刊行会|series=くまの文庫4}} * {{Cite book ja-jp|author=西 律|year=1987|title=熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録|publisher=荒尾成文堂|series=みなもと選書1}} * {{Cite book ja-jp|editor=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|publisher=平凡社|isbn=458213402-5}}
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子}} * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[中辺路]] * [[九十九王子]] : （[[九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)#祓戸王子|祓戸王子]]） - '''湯の峰王子''' - （[[浜王子]]） * [[湯の峰温泉]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ 熊野本宮観光協会] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html 湯の峰温泉] - [http://www.mikumano.net/index.html み熊野ねっと] 内のページ

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[湯の峰王子]] to [[Yunomine Ōji]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
[[Image:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|湯の峰王子社祠]] [[Image:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|王子社鳥居]] '''湯の峰王子'''（ゆのみねおうじ）は[[和歌山県]][[田辺市]]本宮町湯峰にある[[神社]]。[[九十九王子]]のひとつで、[[湯の峰温泉]]の温泉街にほど近い、[[東光寺 (田辺市)|東光寺]]の裏手の丘の上にある。国の[[史跡]]「熊野参詣道」（[[2000年]]〈[[平成]]12年〉11月2日指定）の一部<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=田辺市の指定文化財 －記念物－ |publisher=田辺市教育委員会 |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}</ref>。
== 概要 == 湯の峰王子は九十九王子のひとつに数えられるものの、例えば[[藤原定家]]の参詣記に記述がないなど、中世熊野詣期の参詣記にはほとんど記録されていない。この時期の参詣道が、[[本宮町 (和歌山県)|熊野本宮]]から[[熊野川]]を[[新宮市|新宮]]へと下っており、ルートから外れていること、また、[[熊野本宮大社]]参拝に先立っての湯垢離儀礼による潔斎がこの時期には行われていないためである<ref name="Kumanoji">熊野路編さん委員会[1973: 155]</ref>。
ただ、湯の峰を中世熊野詣の参詣者が訪れなかったわけではない。[[天仁]]2年（[[1109年]]）、[[藤原宗忠]]は、[[熊野三山]]巡拝後に船で本宮に帰着した折に湯の峰に立ち寄り、万病を除く名湯と賞賛している（『[[中右記]]』）が、これは長旅の疲れを癒すためであり、信仰上の意義が存するわけではない。[[1134年]]（[[長承]]3年）には、[[鳥羽天皇|鳥羽院]]の御幸に同行した[[藤原璋子|待賢門院]]は、鳥羽院一行の本宮着が遅れている間に、休養のために湯の峰を訪ねようとしたが、鳥羽院一行が来着したため断念している<ref name="Kumanoji"/>。
史料初出は、鎌倉時代末、[[正中 (元号)|正中]]3年（[[1326年]]）の[[仁和寺]]文書『熊野縁起』に湯峰童子とあるもので、次いで[[文明 (日本)|文明]]5年（[[1474年]]）の『王子記』に「湯峯王子」の記載が見える<ref name="Heibon">平凡社[1997: 794]</ref> が、いずれも中世熊野詣の盛期とは大きく離れている。湯垢離儀礼による潔斎の定着もこの時期以降と考えられており、儀礼の定着に伴って新たに設けられたものと考えられている<ref name="Heibon"/>。その一例として、[[足利義満]]の側室、北野殿らの一行による参詣記に、日中に本宮に到着してから、夜に行われる奉幣の前に湯の峰を訪れている例がある<ref name="Kumanoji"/> ほか、[[熊野本宮大社例大祭]]の湯登神事（毎年4月13日）で今日でも行われている湯の峰温泉での湯垢離は、[[江戸時代]]には記載が見られる（『紀伊続風土記』）<ref name="Heibon"/>。
[[室町時代|室町期]]ころから熊野詣が衰勢し、かわって盛んになった[[西国三十三所]]の一部に中辺路が組み込まれるようになると、参詣者たちは、[[熊野那智大社]]から[[雲取越え]]を行い、温泉での休息後、しばしば[[熊野本宮大社|熊野本宮]]へ立ち寄ることなく[[九十九王子 (田辺市中辺路町)#三越峠と赤木越|赤木越]]経由で[[九十九王子 (田辺市中辺路町)#三越峠と赤木越|三越峠]]へ連絡し、[[田辺市|紀伊田辺]]方面へ直接帰路をとるようになった<ref name="Kumanoji"/><ref name="Nishi">西[1987: 166]</ref>。
近世以降の記録にはときとして名が見られ、『[[紀伊続風土記]]』には社領5石、修造は官営によるとの記述があるほか、本宮大社の管理下にある末社であったとも記されている<ref name="Heibon"/><ref name="Nishi"/>。もとは東光寺の境内、寺堂の右にあったが、[[1903年]]（[[明治36年]]）[[5月]]の火災で、東光寺の本堂ともに焼失、現在地に再建された<ref name="Nishi"/>。
== 交通機関 == * [[紀勢本線]][[新宮駅]]より[[熊野交通]]バスで約60分 * [[紀勢本線]][[紀伊田辺駅]]より[[龍神自動車|龍神バス]]で約90分 * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[大和八木駅]]より、[[奈良交通]]の[[新宮駅]]ゆき特急バス、近鉄[[高田市駅]]、[[近鉄御所駅]]、[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[和歌山線]][[五条駅 (奈良県)|五条駅]]、[[十津川村|十津川]]を経由、約5時間20分 いずれも「湯の峰温泉」バス停で下車
== 所在地 == 和歌山県田辺市本宮町湯峰11
== 注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book ja-jp|author=熊野路編さん委員会|year=1973|title=古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路|publisher=熊野中辺路刊行会|series=くまの文庫4}} * {{Cite book ja-jp|author=西 律|year=1987|title=熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録|publisher=荒尾成文堂|series=みなもと選書1}} * {{Cite book ja-jp|editor=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|publisher=平凡社|isbn=458213402-5}}
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子}} * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[中辺路]] * [[九十九王子]] : （[[九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)#祓戸王子|祓戸王子]]） - '''湯の峰王子''' - （[[浜王子]]） * [[湯の峰温泉]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ 熊野本宮観光協会] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html 湯の峰温泉] - [http://www.mikumano.net/index.html み熊野ねっと] 内のページ

'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}/{{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|lt=Hongū Taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yunomine Ōji}} ==[[Kujūku Ōji template|List of the 99 Oji]]== {{citation needed|湯の峰王子|ill|Jōnan-gū|en}}{{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社|lt=Nachi Taisha|lt=11|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft}} {{Kujūku Ōji}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[en:Yunomine Ōji]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yunomine Ōji}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yunomine Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|湯の峰王子|version=98184098|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}/{{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|lt=Hongū Taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yunomine Ōji}} ==[[Kujūku Ōji template|List of the 99 Oji]]== {{citation needed|湯の峰王子|ill|Jōnan-gū|en}}{{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社|lt=Nachi Taisha|lt=11|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft}} {{Kujūku Ōji}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yunomine Ōji}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yunomine Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|湯の峰王子|version=98184098|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[en:Yunomine Ōji]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]]

'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}/{{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|lt=Hongū Taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} ==[[Kujūku Ōji template|List of the 99 Oji]]== {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社|lt=Nachi Taisha|lt=11|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft}} {{Kujūku Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|湯の峰王子|version=98184098|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[en:Yunomine Ōji]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]]
