'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}/{{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|lt=Hongū Taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} ==[[Kujūku Ōji template|List of the 99 Oji]]== {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社|lt=Nachi Taisha|lt=11|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft}} {{Kujūku Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|湯の峰王子|version=98184098|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[en:Yunomine Ōji]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]]

The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna-ji|qq=}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Station|qq=}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisei Main Line|qq=}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-Tanabe Station|qq=}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-Yagi Station|qq=}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa, Nara|qq=}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe, Wakayama|qq=}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodō|qq=}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}}/{{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|lt=Hongū Taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hongū Taisha|qq=}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|qq=}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} ==[[Kujūku Ōji template|List of the 99 Oji]]== {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社|lt=Nachi Taisha|lt=11|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Nachi Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}} {{Kujūku Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|湯の峰王子|version=98184098|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[en:Yunomine Ōji]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]]

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Kumano Three Shrines]] → [[Kumano shrine]]
revision.text:
'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe, Wakayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodō|qq=}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe, Wakayama|qq=}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|qq=}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodō|qq=}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Teika|qq=}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano River|qq=}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hongū Taisha|qq=}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū, Wakayama|qq=}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna-ji|qq=}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Station|qq=}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisei Main Line|qq=}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-Tanabe Station|qq=}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-Yagi Station|qq=}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa, Nara|qq=}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe, Wakayama|qq=}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodō|qq=}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}}/{{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|lt=Hongū Taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hongū Taisha|qq=}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|qq=}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} ==[[Kujūku Ōji template|List of the 99 Oji]]== {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社|lt=Nachi Taisha|lt=11|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Nachi Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}} {{Kujūku Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|湯の峰王子|version=98184098|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[en:Yunomine Ōji]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]]

The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Station|qq=}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisei Main Line|qq=}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-Tanabe Station|qq=}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-Yagi Station|qq=}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa, Nara|qq=}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe, Wakayama|qq=}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodō|qq=}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}}/{{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|lt=Hongū Taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hongū Taisha|qq=}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|qq=}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} ==[[Template:Kujūku Ōji|List of the 99 Oji]]== {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社|lt=Nachi Taisha|lt=11|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Nachi Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}} {{Kujūku Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|湯の峰王子|version=98184098|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[en:Yunomine Ōji]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "九十九王子|" to "九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|"
revision.text:
'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe, Wakayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodō|qq=}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe, Wakayama|qq=}}, Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|qq=}}, within the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodō|qq=}}, a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|ja|藤原定家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Teika|qq=}}. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano River|qq=}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hongū Taisha|qq=}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū, Wakayama|qq=}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna-ji|qq=}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Station|qq=}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisei Main Line|qq=}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-Tanabe Station|qq=}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-Yagi Station|qq=}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa, Nara|qq=}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe, Wakayama|qq=}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodō|qq=}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}}/{{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|lt=Hongū Taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hongū Taisha|qq=}} → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|湯の峰温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|qq=}} == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} ==[[Template:Kujūku Ōji|List of the 99 Oji]]== {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|ja|熊野那智大社|lt=Nachi Taisha|lt=11|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Nachi Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}} {{Kujūku Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|湯の峰王子|version=98184098|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[en:Yunomine Ōji]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]]

During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano River|qq=}} downstream from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hongū Taisha|qq=}} to {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|ja|新宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū, Wakayama|qq=}}, bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna-ji|qq=}}, with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From {{ill|Shingū Station|ja|新宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Station|qq=}} (JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisei Main Line|qq=}}): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=}} bus * From {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii-Tanabe Station|qq=}}: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=}} * From {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|大和八木駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-Yagi Station|qq=}} (Kintetsu): direct bus via {{ill|Totsukawa, Nara|ja|十津川村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa, Nara|qq=}} (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe, Wakayama|qq=}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodō|qq=}} - revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|qq=}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}/[[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Hongū Taisha]] → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|qq=}} * [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]] == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} ==[[Template:Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|List of the 99 Oji]]== [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Ninety-Nine Ōji]] {{Kujūku Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|湯の峰王子|version=98184098|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[en:Yunomine Ōji]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]]

revision.text:
'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|qq=}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}/[[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Hongū Taisha]] → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|qq=}} * [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]] == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} ==[[Template:Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|List of the 99 Oji]]== [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Ninety-Nine Ōji]] {{Kujūku Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|湯の峰王子|version=98184098|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[en:Yunomine Ōji]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]] [[Category:和歌山県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:田辺市の建築物]]

revision.text:
'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|qq=}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}/[[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Hongū Taisha]] → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|qq=}} * [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]] == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} ==References== [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Ninety-Nine Ōji]] {{Kujūku Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|湯の峰王子|version=98184098|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[en:Yunomine Ōji]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]] [[Category:和歌山県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:田辺市の歴史]]

'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|qq=}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|qq=}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kujūku Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}/[[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Hongū Taisha]] → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|qq=}} * [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]] == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Kujūku Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|湯の峰王子|version=98184098|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujuku-ojis]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujuku-ojis]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[en:Yunomine Ōji]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:田辺市の歴史]]

{{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}/[[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Hongū Taisha]] → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|qq=}} * [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]] == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}}''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|qq=}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}/[[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Hongū Taisha]] → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|qq=}} * [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]] == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}}''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|qq=}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}/[[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Hongū Taisha]] → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|qq=}} * [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]] == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|湯の峰王子|version=98184098|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujuku-ojis]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujuku-ojis]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]]

'''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}}''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|qq=}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}/[[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Hongū Taisha]] → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|qq=}} * [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]] == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|湯の峰王子|version=98184098|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]]

The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野御坊南海バス}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 龍神自動車}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] -
