熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|湯の峰王子|version=98184098|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]]

The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野御坊南海バス}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 龍神自動車}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}/[[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Hongū Taisha]] → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|qq=}} * [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]] == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|湯の峰王子|version=98184098|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563413]] Wikidata name: Yunomine-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}} | image = [[File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Yunomine Ōji Shrine]]
[[File:Sacred arch YUNOMINE-oji.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii of Yunomine Ōji]] | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|49|45|N|135|45|30|E|region:JP|display=title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = }} '''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=}}''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|qq=}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000. == Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine. The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha. The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall. == Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野御坊南海バス}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 龍神自動車}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop. == Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}/[[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Hongū Taisha]] → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|qq=}} * [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]] == References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社. == External Links == * [http://www.hongu.jp/yunomine2/ Kumano Hongū Tourist Association] * [http://www.mikumano.net/meguri/yunomine.html Yunomine Onsen at Mikumano.net] {{Shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|湯の峰王子|version=98184098|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Yunomine Ōji]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[ja:湯の峰王子]] [[zh:湯の峰王子]] [[sn:unomine Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:湯の峰王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563413]] Wikidata name: Yunomine-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}} | image = File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.829167|N|135.758333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Tanabe|qid=Q304266}} | part_of = {{ill|Kujūku-ōji|qid=Q3200280}} }}
'''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}}''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|qq=|1=Tōkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526024}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000.
== Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine.
The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha.
The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野御坊南海バス|1=Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568871}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 龍神自動車|1=Ryūjin Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679334}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop.
== Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|qq=|1=Nakahechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30074932}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}/[[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Hongū Taisha]] → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|qq=|1=Hama Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557908}}
* [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]]
== References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:湯の峰王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563413]] Wikidata name: Yunomine-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}} | image = File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.829167|N|135.758333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Tanabe|WD=Q304266}} | part_of = {{ill|Kujūku-ōji|WD=Q3200280}} }}
'''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|qq=|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}}''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|qq=|1=Tōkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526024}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000.
== Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine.
The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha.
The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野御坊南海バス|1=Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568871}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 龍神自動車|1=Ryūjin Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679334}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:湯の峰王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563413]] Wikidata name: Yunomine-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}} | image = File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.829167|N|135.758333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Tanabe|WD=Q304266}} | part_of = {{ill|Kujūku-ōji|WD=Q3200280}} }}
'''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}}''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|1=Tōkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526024}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000.
== Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine.
The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha.
The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野御坊南海バス|1=Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568871}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 龍神自動車|1=Ryūjin Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679334}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop.
== Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|1=Nakahechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30074932}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}/[[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Hongū Taisha]] → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|1=Hama Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557908}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:湯の峰王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563413]] Wikidata name: Yunomine-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}} | image = File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.829167|N|135.758333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Tanabe|WD=Q304266}} | part_of = {{ill|Kujūku-ōji|WD=Q3200280}} }}
'''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}}''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|1=Tōkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526024}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000.
== Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine.
The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha.
The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野御坊南海バス|1=Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568871}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 龍神自動車|1=Ryūjin Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679334}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:湯の峰王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563413]] Wikidata name: Yunomine-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}} | image = File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.829167|N|135.758333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Tanabe|WD=Q304266}} | part_of = {{ill|Kujūku-ōji|WD=Q3200280}} }}
'''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}}''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|1=Tōkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526024}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000.
== Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine.
The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha.
The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野御坊南海バス|1=Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568871}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 龍神自動車|1=Ryūjin Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679334}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop.
== Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|1=Nakahechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30074932}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}/[[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Hongū Taisha]] → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|1=Hama Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557908}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:湯の峰王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563413]] Wikidata name: Yunomine-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}} | image = File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.829167|N|135.758333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Tanabe|WD=Q304266}} | part_of = {{ill|Kujūku-ōji|WD=Q3200280}} }}
'''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}}''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|1=Tōkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526024}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000.
== Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine.
The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha.
The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野御坊南海バス|1=Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568871}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 龍神自動車|1=Ryūjin Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679334}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop.
== Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|1=Nakahechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30074932}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}/[[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Hongū Taisha]] → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|1=Hama Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557908}}
* [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]]
== References == * 熊野路編さん委員会 (1973). *古道と王子社 - 熊野中辺路*. 熊野中辺路刊行会. * 西 律 (1987). *熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録*. 荒尾成文堂. * 平凡社 (1997). *大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典*. 平凡社.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:湯の峰王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563413]] Wikidata name: Yunomine-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}} | image = File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.829167|N|135.758333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Tanabe|WD=Q304266}} | part_of = {{ill|Kujūku-ōji|WD=Q3200280}} }}
'''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}}''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|1=Tōkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526024}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000.
== Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine.
The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha.
The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野御坊南海バス|1=Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568871}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 龍神自動車|1=Ryūjin Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679334}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop.
== Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|1=Nakahechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30074932}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}/[[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Hongū Taisha]] → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|1=Hama Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557908}}
* [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:湯の峰王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563413]] Wikidata name: Yunomine-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}} | image = File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.829167|N|135.758333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Tanabe|WD=Q304266}} | part_of = {{ill|Kujūku-ōji|WD=Q3200280}} }}
'''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}}''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|1=Tōkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526024}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000.
== Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine.
The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha.
The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野御坊南海バス|1=Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568871}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 龍神自動車|1=Ryūjin Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679334}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:湯の峰王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563413]] Wikidata name: Yunomine-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}} | image = File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.829167|N|135.758333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Tanabe|WD=Q304266}} | part_of = {{ill|Kujūku-ōji|WD=Q3200280}} }}
'''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}}''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|1=Tōkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526024}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000.
== Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine.
The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha.
The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野御坊南海バス|1=Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568871}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 龍神自動車|1=Ryūjin Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679334}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:湯の峰王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563413]] Wikidata name: Yunomine-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}} | image = File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.829167|N|135.758333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Tanabe|WD=Q304266}} | part_of = {{ill|Kujūku-ōji|WD=Q3200280}} }}
'''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}}''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|1=Tōkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526024}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000.
== Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine.
The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha.
The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野御坊南海バス|1=Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568871}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 龍神自動車|1=Ryūjin Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679334}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop.
== Address == 11 Yunomine, Hongū-chō, [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|1=Nakahechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30074932}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ** {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|en|lt=Haraido Ōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Hongū Town)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}/[[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Hongū Taisha]] → '''Yunomine Ōji''' → {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Ōji|1=Hama Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557908}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:湯の峰王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563413]] Wikidata name: Yunomine-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yunomine Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}} | image = File:YUNOMINE-oji shrine.jpg | location = [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.829167|N|135.758333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Tanabe|WD=Q304266}} | part_of = {{ill|Kujūku-ōji|WD=Q3200280}} }}
'''{{ill|Yunomine Ōji|ja|湯の峰王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Ōji|1=Yunomine Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563413}}''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, located in [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]], [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]], Japan. It is situated on a hill behind {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|ja|東光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Tanabe)|1=Tōkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526024}}, within the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], a designated [[National Historic Site of Japan]] since 2000.
== Overview == Despite being part of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, Yunomine Ōji is rarely mentioned in medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]. During the height of the Kumano pilgrimage, routes primarily followed the [[:en:Kumano River|Kumano River]] downstream from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] to [[:en:Shingu|Shingū, Wakayama]], bypassing Yunomine.
The earliest historical reference appears in a 1326 document from [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]], with more mentions in the 15th century. The shrine likely became significant later due to the practice of ritual purification in hot springs (湯垢離, *yu-gori*) before worship at Kumano Hongū Taisha.
The current shrine was rebuilt after a fire in 1903 destroyed the original structure along with Tōkō-ji's main hall.
== Transportation == * From [[:en:Shingū Station|Shingū Station]] (JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]]): approx. 60 minutes by {{ill|Kumano Kōtsu|ja|熊野交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōtsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野御坊南海バス|1=Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568871}} bus * From [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]]: approx. 90 minutes by {{ill|Ryujin Bus|ja|龍神バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujin Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 龍神自動車|1=Ryūjin Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679334}} * From [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]] (Kintetsu): direct bus via [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa, Nara]] (approx. 5 hr 20 min) * All buses stop at "Yunomine Onsen" bus stop.
